message.content:
You are a SaaS teardown expert. Analyse these competitor websites for a UK trades SaaS called JobFilter. Be brutally honest. No fluff. Bullet points only.

FETCH THESE SITES (use WebFetch on each):
- https://buildscout.co.uk
- https://quickbase.com
- https://rentman.io
- https://zenzap.app
- https://hivesuite.app
- https://siteconnect.io
- https://myramsapp.com
- https://framework.construction
- https://demo.zypheragents.co.uk
- https://constructionai.io

FOR EACH SITE, extract:
1. Hero headline (exact words)
2. Primary CTA text
3. How they show the product (screenshot, animation, dashboard UI, none)
4. Trust signals used (stats, logos, testimonials, reviews)
5. Color palette feel (1 line)
6. Whether it feels "real SaaS" or "fake/demo" — and WHY

THEN answer:
- What patterns do the real-feeling ones share that JobFilter lacks?
- What does demo.zypheragents.co.uk do better than a typical lead-gen site?
- What 5 specific things must JobFilter copy to feel like a real product?
- What 3 things must JobFilter avoid?

JobFilter context: black/yellow aggressive construction aesthetic, all-caps screaming, no product visuals, demo page feels like a dev tool, mixes B2B manufacturer product (Codex) with B2C trades product on same nav.

Output: structured bullet list, brutal and specific.

input.prompt: Extract: 1) Hero headline exact words, 2) Primary CTA button text, 3) How product is shown (screenshot/animation/dashboard/none), 4) Trust signals (stats/logos/testimonials/reviews), 5) Color palette description, 6) Overall feel real SaaS or fake/demo

content.text:
WebFetch permission is blocked in this session. To unblock it, you can run this in your Claude Code terminal:

```
/permissions allow WebFetch
```

Or via settings: run `/settings` and add WebFetch to the allowed tools list.

Once you grant that permission, I can fetch all 10 sites simultaneously and run the full teardown. Here is exactly what is blocked and why it matters:

- WebFetch is the tool that lets me retrieve and parse live web pages
- Without it, I cannot read hero copy, CTAs, UI patterns, or trust signals from any of those URLs
- I have the full analysis framework ready to execute the moment the permission is granted

To grant it quickly, type `/permissions allow WebFetch` in the Claude Code prompt and then resend your message. I will run all 10 fetches in parallel and deliver the full structured teardown.
